svg.font-family: Inter, Segoe UI, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: Private AI Code Assistant on Red Hat OpenShift
VS Code for the Web (Browser-Based IDE)
Runs inside a workspace pod with persistent storage and terminal access
Extensions call the llm-d Gateway using
TLS termination and request routing rules
Envoy ext_proc filter invokes EPP for endpoint selection
A single custom resource (CR) automatically provisions:
Kubernetes internal DNS resolves to ClusterIP. Inference traffic
never crosses the cluster boundary or reaches the public internet.
EPP uses real-time metrics (KV-cache, prefix-cache, queue depth)
to route each request to the optimal replica. No round-robin.
Different models on different accelerators (NVIDIA, Inferentia, Trainium)
served behind a single gateway endpoint, transparent to clients.
TLS termination at Envoy Gateway. Mutual TLS available via
Service Mesh for encrypted pod-to-pod communication.
One LLMInferenceService CR creates the entire serving stack:
deployment, service, routing, EPP, and gateway binding.
Envoy routes to selected vLLM pod
Red Hat OpenShift AI · KServe · llm-d · vLLM · OpenShift Dev Spaces · Service Mesh
